result.text:
Pretty incredible to see how many businesses are purchasing their own Tesla Superchargers.

They own them, and Tesla manages and maintains them for a small fee.

A site like this would cost around $940,000, but it could pay for itself in just 7 years with an average yearly revenue of $732,653 over 15 years. This doesn’t even count the additional revenue of bringing customers into your actual business.

result.text:
TL;DR 0️⃣ The Best Process is No Process: Tesla's Thermal Shortcut to Skip Cathode Milling 🔥

🔋 Making high quality battery powder usually requires a slow and expensive grinding process to ensure every part of the battery is exactly the same.

⚖️ The main problem is that raw lithium comes in giant chunks while the other ingredients are tiny grains, making it almost impossible to mix them evenly without first crushing the lithium into dust.

💡 Tesla’s invention introduces an intermediate homogenization step that uses heat to melt and spread the lithium across the precursor particles before they react and fuse.

🧱 This move changes battery making from a mechanical grinding job into a smart heating process, which saves energy and keeps the factory floor much simpler.

🔥 By using a specific temperature sequence, the factory first dries out the ingredients and then makes the lithium liquid enough to wrap around every single grain of powder.

🌀 The mixture is stirred slowly and carefully to bring the ingredients together without accidentally smashing or damaging the delicate grains that hold the battery power.

🔬 Tests show that this heating method creates a battery powder that is just as uniform and high quality as the old, more expensive way of doing things.

⚡ Cells built with this process match the performance of expensive milled benchmarks, achieving the high capacity needed for long range electric vehicles.

🧾 The process yields acceptable residual lithium levels, ensuring the final material is stable and ready for high volume electrode coating.

🧩 This manufacturing logic works across diverse chemistries including high energy nickel cells and low cost iron phosphate batteries, making it a versatile tool for the entire Tesla lineup.

🚀 By applying this to a massive factory, Tesla can save thousands of tons of material and potentially build enough extra batteries to power thousands of more vehicles every year.

result.preview_text:
Before a lithium-ion cell becomes a 4680, before it becomes a pack, and long before it moves a car, it begins as powder.
That powder has to be controlled with almost obsessive precision. If lithium is
result.title: 🔋 Tesla has found a way to skip lithium milling without wrecking cathode quality 🔬

Warning: This is not investment advice.

Last quarter, Donald Trump bought millions in stock across 15 companies.

These aren't just any companies. They're giants in tech, finance, and manufacturing, sectors that have a massive influence on our daily lives.

Soon after, the CEOs of all 15 firms were invited to join him on an official state visit to China:

Jensen Huang (Nvidia)

Kelly Ortberg (Boeing)

Elon Musk (Tesla)

Larry Fink (BlackRock)

Mark Zuckerberg (Meta)

David Solomon (Goldman Sachs)

Stephen Schwarzman (Blackstone)

Jane Fraser (Citi)

Jim Anderson (Coherent)

Larry Culp (GE Aerospace)

Michael Miebach (Mastercard)

Sanjay Mehrotra (Micron)

Cristiano Amon (Qualcomm)

Tim Cook (Apple)

Ryan McInerney (Visa)

Coincidence? You decide.

legacy.full_text: Many refer to the Tesla community here on X as family, and I’d say that’s 💯 🎯 accurate. I was looking for a Tesla to visit my mom in San Diego while I’m in LA for work, and @JHS19701 and @summerz1Amy stepped up without hesitation. They are two of the most amazing people I’ve https://t.co/LVY0f7yY9g

result.text:
Many refer to the Tesla community here on X as family, and I’d say that’s 💯 🎯 accurate. I was looking for a Tesla to visit my mom in San Diego while I’m in LA for work, and @JHS19701 and @summerz1Amy stepped up without hesitation. They are two of the most amazing people I’ve met from X! 

Thank you from the bottom of my heart! It was great meeting you both, and I look forward to keeping in contact with you both. Hearts of gold, cool, and willing to trust a leadfoot like me with their MYLR (thankfully FSD was near 100%, so no worries 😉) - what’s not to love?! 

The best thing about my travels are meeting people all over the country who I interact with on X. I’ve not once been disappointed. Well, maybe with @JCChristopher 😂 😝 But seriously, we all should take the time to meet our neighbors on X. 

So if you haven’t signed up for or plan to attend @theXtakeover in October, DO IT! You’ll meet people like Amy and JHS, and many others who make this community awesome!
